We are given the 4x4 matrix:

$$
A = \begin{bmatrix}
-5 & -5 & 5 & 1 \\
-3 & -3 & -4 & -1 \\
-4 & 1 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
$$

We are to compute the **determinant** of this matrix.

---

### Step 1: Use cofactor expansion (along the first row)

The determinant of a 4x4 matrix can be computed using cofactor expansion along the first row:

$$
\det(A) = -5 \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -1 \\
1 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
+ (-5) \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -1 \\
-4 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
+ 5 \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -1 \\
-4 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 2 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
+ 1 \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -1 \\
-4 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 2 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
$$

Let’s compute each 3x3 determinant step by step.

---

### Step 2: Compute the first 3x3 determinant

$$
D_1 = \det\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -1 \\
1 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
$$

Using cofactor expansion along the first row:

$$
D_1 = -3 \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
1 & 4 \\
4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
+ (-4) \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
1 & 4 \\
2 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
+ (-1) \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
1 & 1 \\
2 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
$$
